description: Add DELETE /users/:id that soft-deletes a user by setting a deletedAt timestamp on the record (do not remove from the array). After deletion, GET /users should exclude soft-deleted records, and GET /users/:id should return 404 for deleted users.
expected_behavior: DELETE /users/1 sets db.users[0].deletedAt = new Date(). GET /users excludes it. GET /users/1 returns 404.

success_criteria.description: Sets a deletedAt field rather than removing from the array

success_criteria.rubric: Does the GET /users handler filter out records where deletedAt is set? Is the filter applied before returning the list?
Does the GET /users/:id handler treat a record with deletedAt set the same as a missing record (404 response)?
